description:
# Don't compile dead specs

## The smell

ast_eval's main calls a fully-narrowed spec via `return_call fn5`;
`eval(any)` is **never called at runtime** in this program, yet we
compile and ship its body. Same shape for `none`-typed continuation
specs that exist as typer-iteration artifacts and aren't reachable
from any callsite.

## What changes

After typer settles, before codegen iterates specs to emit fn
bodies, do a reachability walk:

1. Build a (FnId, Vec<Descr>) → set of (FnId, Vec<Descr>) edge map
   from the typer's spec set. Edges come from direct Call /
   TailCall / CallClosure / TailCallClosure / MakeClosure /
   Receive cont sites in each spec's body. The typer already
   walks these sites in `walk_spec_for_discovery` — refactor or
   mirror that traversal.
2. Seed BFS from:
     - (main_fn_id, []) when main exists
     - Every exported entry's any-key
     - Every spec referenced by static_closure_targets
3. Walk; record reached spec ids.
4. In compile_with_backend's spec emission loop, **skip** specs
   not in the reached set. They stay in the registry (for
   id-numbering stability and resolve subsumption) but emit no
   fn body and no symbol.

## What's removed

- Code emission for unreached specs (eval(any) when never
  called; none-typed iteration artifacts).
- Their entries in the emitted object's symbol table.
- ~hundreds of lines from ast_eval/expected.clif (estimated:
  drops eval(any) + 4 k_NN any-key fns + any none-typed remnants
  that fz-ul4.40 didn't already prevent).

## What stays

- The spec registry layout, ids, and subsumption-dispatch
  semantics. We're not removing specs from the registry — only
  from codegen.
- Sentinel-slot skip behavior (already in place).

## Atomic steps

1. Extract or share the body-walk traversal that
   `walk_spec_for_discovery` already does.
2. Add a `reachable_specs(&module_types, &module) -> HashSet<SpecId>`
   function and unit-test it.
3. Thread the set into compile_with_backend; gate the per-sid
   emit loop.
4. Re-bless ast_eval; verify other fixture goldens unchanged
   (no existing fixture should have dead specs — verify).

## Acceptance

- ast_eval golden shrinks further; `eval(any)` and analogous
  dead specs gone.
- All 154 existing fixture matrix trials pass; their goldens
  byte-identical (no fixture today has unreachable specs —
  verify).
- Unit test for `reachable_specs` covering: main-only program,
  closure-via-MakeClosure, mutual recursion.

## Risk

A spec we think is dead might actually be reached via a path the
walk missed (e.g. mid-flight resume shims, scheduler hooks,
test entries). Mitigation: be **conservative** — when uncertain,
mark reachable. Easy to tighten later.

## Out of scope

- Removing the dead specs from the registry itself (renumbering
  SpecIds would cascade through frame schemas and metadata).
- Cross-module dead-code elimination.
- Inliner cleanup.

comments.text:
**Cleanup hand-off from fz-ul4.40 v1.**

fz-ul4.40 v1 (PR #13) added `skip_payoff_free_spec` in `src/ir_typer.rs` as a chokepoint filter that drops candidate specs whose key contains a `Descr::is_empty()` element. That filter exists *only* because the typer currently produces such candidates — which it does because cont_input_key reads slot 0 from `effective_returns` of the callee, and unreachable callees contribute `none`.

fz-ul4.42 (this ticket) prunes unreachable specs at the source. Post-42, no callee is typed unless reachable from main/exports, so no `none` slot 0 contributions, so no `none`-keyed cont candidates, so no work for `skip_payoff_free_spec` to do.

**Acceptance addition**: closing this ticket should include deleting `skip_payoff_free_spec` and its call site in `drain_and_register` (`src/ir_typer.rs`). Verify ast_eval and full fixture matrix still pass post-deletion — if they don't, 42's reachability set is incomplete and needs widening (not a v1 gate restoration).

This is the data-model-correctness layering: 42 removes the bad data at its source, making the boundary filter redundant. Per the design discussion, keeping the v1 gate temporary and deleting it cleanly when 42 lands is cheaper than implementing a middle-layer push-site filter that would also need deletion.

title: codegen: skip emission of specs unreachable from main
